channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: On the Psychology of Long-term Bagholders — 22 replies — 88.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
On the Psychology of Long-term Bagholders

Open thread • Replies: 22 • Updated: 2026-05-27 17:15 UTC

OP

Any good reads on the (patho)psychology of long term bag holders? GME, BBY, LINK, Bayhorse, any other of the many, many shitcoins. I find these groups very fun and interesting to observe, and I'm wondering if there have been any good scholarly works on these populations. What makes them tick? What blinds them so? Why must the hold? How can I ensure I avoid such a fate? How can I help friends of similar delusions?

Early replies (first 3)

62275443
>>62275427
It’s the sunk cost fallacy played out. Some people would rather become “part of the community” instead of taking the L and moving on.

62275453
>>62275443
That's a good point re community. I'd imagine many don't have other communities to be a part of and/or check them, so there is value to being part of this group, even if it costs you financially.

62275567
>>62275427
So you mean like normal investing. Buying a stock and being long on it. Do you also sneed about people who stacked silver for years like a retard? sage

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62275610
>>62275427
Hope bias, sunken cost fallacy, they learned to disregard any information not from their bubble as fud, self supported more and more unrealistic narratives in their bubble.

62277126
>>62275427
im one. i recognize I'm retarded, I also think I understand the value proposition of chain link. Being retarded, yet "understanding the value proposition" has trapped me because I know with %100 certainty I'm too retarded to make it any other way than holding link

62277147
>>62275427
Link actually does something, none of those other things do anything. Despite having the largest integrations and usecase in the entirety of crypto for some reason Links price doesn't move though. Everything I've just said is also 100% undeniable fact. The only difference between people acknowledging Link is the price moving or not. It moves and suddely every integration is real and investors were genius all along.

62277276
>>62275567
CHAINLINK =\= legit investment

62277336
>>62275427
Don't forget xrp

62277352
>>62275567
Are we really comparing de nice Chainlink token which is used for nothing to silver?

62277389
>>62275427
I hold a green position in one of these. Why should I sell or justify it to some 0dte indiafag?

62277527
For Link, many people are in the green but holding out for at least a 10x or something. Link spent multiple years trading around 5 dollars and the ATH run up it had before was incredibly short so I doubt there's even any bagholders left who went in at that price. I'd understand why people wouldn't want to walk away with a measly 2x, imagine how shitty that would feel to miss a giant pump after. I still have around 1000 link I'm holding onto just in case, not particularly worried because a 2x on that is practically nothing, and I diversified into other cryptos, ETF's and PM's so my livelihood doesn't hinge on the success of one project.

62277546
>>62277126
I'm gonna give you a spoiler, you won't EVER make it with link, even if you hold it for 30 years. Move on faggot.

>>62277527
Another spoiler, there won't be be any pump in the next bull, expect link sub $20, perhaps sub $15. People don't understand basic tokenomics and inflationary assets.

62278879
>>62275427
hey uhhh
im gonna take all of your and your bosses money
you are wrong, been wrong since 2012, arguably 2008, you bum niggas been shorting without cessation since
sorry you had to find out like this. I also will feel nothing watching bankers rain.

62278882
>>62275427
Id love to read about the psycjology of people who spam an obscure finance board fudding LINK for over half a decade

62278936
Holding XRP for 7 years forces you to reality-check on a regular basis. On one hand, I have no arguments against looking like every group mentioned in OP. But at the same time, Ripple has laid down roots in the highest places and continues to do so. The new Fed guy, Kevin Warsh, wrote a paper on how XRP is the liquidity solution for stablecoins. And yet... price?

I could dump everything right now or baghold until Armageddon and feel equally justified in either case.

62279696
>>62275427
I'm a longterm baggie of BTC.

I hodl because every other investment I've tried has fucked me over hard. I can leave it in BTC and at least I know Wall Street and the government can't fuck me over.

62279853
>>62278936
This.
I’ve held through the worst of it during the biden years and the bullshit the sec put us through, this isn’t nearly as bad as that period of lawfare. It is still tiresome though seeing how low the price is with all these good things happening.

62279864
>>62275427
The idea of holding something for 5+ years only for it to moon shortly after you sell is so terrifying and pretty much guaranteed to lead to suicide, so hitting the sell button is basically playing russian roulette

62279986
It takes grace, humility, and courage to admit you were wrong. Unfortunately most just double down and become shills, roping as many others as they can into the quagmire of their own delusions and greed.

62279995
>>62277546
>tokenomics
I can’t not envision anyone who uses this word as an argument as an Indian.
There’s only one form in digital scarcity and that’s BTC. Everything else is a slow-bleeding scam.

62280262
>>62275427
A long term holder either know what he is doing, or do not know what he is doing.
A long term BTC holder who knows exactly why he is a long term BTC holder is smart.
A long term shitcoin, non POW coin holder who think that his project is good, when it actually already failed, is delusional.
In the end, remember that the crypto space will play like the dot-com bubble space. A handfull projects will skyrocket. The vast majority will fail into oblivion, being part of a nostalgic era that is long gone.
The shitcoin holder has the same mentality as the trumpist who still think in may 2026 that Trump will drain the swamp. Delusional psychology.

62280300
>>62275427
Youve been duped. Those are all bot threads. They paid 100 dollars to an estonian for a decades worth of general threads. The threads outlast the scam.

item.title: What’s stopping you from buying BTC? — 91 replies — 85.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
What’s stopping you from buying BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 91 • Updated: 2026-05-27 16:21 UTC

OP

What is it? Fear? Lack of conviction? Waiting for lower prices? Or do you genuinely think Bitcoin fails long term?

Curious what’s holding people back at this stage.

Early replies (first 3)

62271721
>>62271712
Btc needs a catalyst. It probably will be yield curve control with a new name. It could also be codifying the SBR into law which is less likely. Or my personal favorite schizo theory, it's discovered that Iran was mining and selling bitcoin to fund it's endeavors and that their supply is finally exhausted.

62271922
>>62271712
Gays in it.

62271933
>>62271712
everybody and their femboy lovers above two-digits IQ are waiting for BTC to dump below at least 40k by July. Why anybody buy now? 4 years cycles never lie

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62273812
>>62272874
>moving huge wealth out of a country
Only brown people leave their home country. Only criminals do tax evasion. I'm neither of those.

62273814
>>62271712
lack of desire to throw my money away to a kike scam

62273819
>>62271935
Ok but a lot depends on your risk tolerance and what kind of returns you’re expecting from this stage of the market.

62273824
>>62272659
>too bad you didnt buy at $200 or you'd be retired
retired on what? US dollars from selling your shitty worthless digital ponzi coin? so you admit bitcoin has no purpose or use other than hoping other retards buy it to pump up the price so you can exit before it crashes?

62273959
>>62273824
So you're buying stocks in the hope some retards will pump the price so you can exit before it crashes?

62273985
>>62271712
The narrative keeps shifting. First it was supposed to finally defeat the dollar, then it was supposed to be ATMs everywhere, then it's supposed to be digital gold, then the government and blackrock buy it, then it's not SUPPOSED to be in atms and then it's just really a leverage tech stock without any tech.

It's insanity. How am I supposed to know what the price will be in 10 years if the narrative cycle is like in 2?

62273994
>>62273787
This guy who spams this in every discussion has convinced me that what he's talking about is a scam and I'll never use it.

62274011
>>62271712
I have a little bitcoin, but I need large amounts of cash on hand.

62274038
>>62273994
>DYOR
>he does not DYOR
Doesn't matter that much though since you can just keep scaling by putting gains into new assets rather than ever staking.

62274120
Let's buy more BTC

62274282
>>62273959
yes beause stocks represnt ownership in a company which is a real asset with actual cashflow unlike a digital ponzi based on a generic internet token with zero utility that anyone can make at any time

also im not pretending stocks are the future currency of the world that everyone is going to use like the retard dipshits such as yourself

62274315
>>62273655
>i dont care if it goes to 1million
the /biz/ way

62274703
>>62273994
You didn’t even look into how it works and already calling it a scam lol. At least research it first before getting emotional.

62274705
>>62271712
It needs to go down to $50, so I will buy them.

otherwise GTFO!

62274708
>>62271960
Most people only believe in BTC after the price already moves far beyond what they once thought was impossible.

62275419
>>62271712
I am always buying BTC.

62275451
I don't have tens of thousands of dollars to blow on just one type of investment. Sorry I noticed too late and was too financially illiterate to trust it over a decade ago.

62275467
>>62271712
>make gains elsewhere
>rotate into BTC
That's how I have to go about it being such a latecomer.

62275501
>>62271712
returns are too low at this point, and it can go to 0 at any time. why are you holding bags?

62276869
>>62272002
BTC doesn’t need to become the global reserve currency overnight to justify current valuations. You people still think adoption is binary for some reason.

62276880
>>62272017
CLARITY isn't going to happen, and the Strategic Bitcoin Reserve won't mean shit unless it is passed as a bill in Congress (which also won't happen)

62276888
Fear
I actually think crypto seems retarded. I also don't understand what makes Bitcoin any different than the 10,000 other shitcoins out there. Yeah Bitcoin was first, but that is the only think I can understand that makes it special compared to numerous other coins.

I feel extreme regret for not buying it. I just make myself feel better by telling myself I would have sold it when I hit my first 5x and I never would have been able to hodl this long.

I literally almost bought $100 of Bitcoin when it was like $5 each. I closed the window at the last moment because I thought it was stupid. It feels like throwing away a winning lottery card whenever I think about it.

62278410
>>62276888
You just didnt make money on crypto

62278419
>>62276880
Retard. Stop smoking

62278837
>>62274703
How you can say it's legit?

62278840
>>62275501
can go to zero? In what way

62278849
>>62274705
Delusional joker

62278858
>>62273985
It had always been fishy.

62280044
>>62278837
You can literally verify everything yourself. We’re way past the point where major players like Aave, Ledger, GoMining, a16z and others would touch obvious garbage for no reason.

62280052
>>62272017
A lot of people still view the market through old cycle assumptions.

item.title: NOT A DRILL — 17 replies — 68.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
NOT A DRILL

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-05-27 17:25 UTC

OP

YouTube is demonetizing automated channels left and right. Youtube automation was supposed to be a stable source of passive income and now people are losing their empires. Why did Google push AI if they were going to punish people for using AI?

Early replies (first 3)

62277230
>>62277227
Cry me a river, dickface.

62277233
>>62277227
Saar...

62277354
>>62277227
>Why did Google push AI if they were going to punish people for using AI?
They needed people to upload AI slop for that data to be fed into the algorithms to train them to punish that content.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62278141
>>62277227
VLC should sue.

62278168
>>62277227
>you can't make money by showing people content they like because you made it with an easy to use tool that produces good content that people like!!! okay???
shorting goog tomorrow

62278201
>>62277227
Content creation is on the chopping block to be automated. People and agents will do it for free. No more payouts to content creators. More profit for GOOG. It’s pretty obvious, really.

62278236
>>62278168
Nobody likes AI slop, AI schizo. Also, not buying your AI bags, you're on your own.

62278254
>>62278236
"Nobody wants a car" said the horse

62278401
>>62278254
I want to watch actual videos of whores and cats, not fake slop created by jeets.

62278404
>>62277227
get a real job

62278414
What about the AI ads?

62278783
>>62278401
AI Cat weapon doorcam videos are better than any real cat video ever made.

62280180
>>62278404
Being an influencer is a real job

62280218
>>62277227
>inauthentic content
HAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAH
Got 'em!

As someone who makes 100% original videos for fun, this makes me happy. I don't make money, I do get a decent number of views. perhaps this will change things.
I ran some youtube theory through a few AI models. AI predicts more wholesome unpolished "real" content will get popular. This try hard, insincerity "Youtuber" "influencer" model is fading.

62280279
Huge news for India

62280285
thank god. I kept getting recommended this climbing channel that would document 100% hallucinated climbing disasters as if they really happened. all of the stories were framed as anti-western morality tales. probably prompted by a resentful jeet.

62280342
>Nooooo now I have to do my own voiceovers on my YouTube videos instead of using AI TTS and nobody is going to listen anymore once they hear my Indian accent nooooooooo

item.title: Just finished my first year of law school and I did really well, so I'm basically a lawyer now. — 41 replies — 57.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Just finished my first year of law school and I did really well, so I'm basically a lawyer now.

Open thread • Replies: 41 • Updated: 2026-05-27 16:42 UTC

OP

Just finished my first year of law school and I did really well, so I'm basically a lawyer now. AMA law related /biz/ questions you may have.

Early replies (first 3)

62272819
>>62272812
Can i peepee in the poopoo or will i go to jail?

62272833
>>62272819
You may

62272835
>>62272812
Is it legal for a president to collab with a news outlet to pump & dump the stock market while insiders make bank on it?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62273090
>>62273047
First year was very basic, we had one oral argument in our legal writing class and that was it. Other than that all classes were just like normal undergrad classes, just law related. There is optional stuff like a Mock Trial tournament every year but they don't make people do that.

62273152
>>62272812
>AMA law related
How much upwards mobility do you think a law degree gives someone? I heard that the rich kids are setup by their parents with the chancery and all while the poor kids struggle to make mich out of their degree.

62273154
>>62272812
what will happen with chainlink on 6/6/26?

62273163
>>62273152
Well I haven't graduated yet, but employment data for my school is really good. It all depends on where you go and how well you do it seems.
>>62273154
It will skyrocket from $9.44 to $9.45

62273171
does it seem like most of your classmates are smart? I was at a high school reunion recently and spoke to a guy who loafed around doing menial work for like 15 years after college. then he took the lsat and got into Northwestern. he has a big time job now defending people in trouble with the SEC.

62273174
>>62273171
Yeah, how many law students are just dumbfucks?

62273234
Junior lawyer is literally technician in legal system. Very far from management and get treated like disposable human trash

62273383
>>62272812
be like this guy anon
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=G-6eynsaZ0U&t=836s

62273401
>>62272812
Should I become a lawyer? My college is paid for and I feel like it'll be easy with AI now

62273402
>>62273041
The Irish are better writers the British

62273648
>>62273171
>>62273174
Nobody I'd call dumb but some came across as pretty lazy. Also AI use is absurd.
>>62273401
It's worth it as long as you don't go to a predatory school and don't drown in debt. What really matters is your LSAT and undergrad GPA. You can use this site to put in your undergrad GPA and see what different LSAT scores will get you into and how much in scholarships. It's mostly LSAT though that determines it, but there's a lot of free LSAT test prep stuff online and it is a very learnable test.
https://lsd.law/law-school-chances-predictor

62273654
Lawyers will be replaced with A.I.

62275142
>>62273654
Sort of like how your dad was replaced by my penis

62275176
>>62272812
Tits or ass

62275435
>>62272812

Are you going to be an actual trial attorney with balls or be a paper pushing cuck like most?

I can't make up my mind who's intelligence the general public overrates more between doctors and lawyers.

62275484
>>62272812
What was your LSAT score? What is your school’s rank? What was your GPA? Where are you interning this summer?

How big is your pp?

62275816
>>62275176
Ass for most of my life but as I’ve gotten older, tits
>>62275435
I honestly don’t know yet, it’s only been one year and I’m not even dead set on which area of the law I want to practice in.
>>62275484
>What was your LSAT score?
170
>What is your school’s rank?
Mid 20s
>What was your GPA?
In undergrad or 1L? In undergrad it was a 3.4 in an easy major, I just didn’t give a shit in college. In 1L it was a 3.5
>Where are you interning this summer?
I’m RAing with a professor and doing a one month internship with a local judge later in the summer.

>How big is your pp?
5.8 inches massive

62275842
Do you ever consider yourself a second class profession compared to finance chads who value and trade and make money off the silly little financial obligation contracts lawyers write up?

62275952
>>62275842
Again dude I just finished my first out of three years of law school, so I don’t know what the actual practice looks like at all.

62276043
>>62275952
I'm just ribbing you buddy, a little white collar banter if you will

62277022
>>62275816
>170 LSAT

Acceptable.

>Mid 20s Law School

Acceptable.

>3.5 1L GPA

Acceptable.

>I’m RAing with a professor and doing a one month internship with a local judge later in the summer.

It’s over. You are cooked. BIG oof. Thanks for playing.

62277039
>>62277022
Why is it over? And why didn’t you address my penis size?

62277080
>>62277039
If you are not interning at an AMLAW 50 firm your 1L summer then you literally need to kill yourself.

[spoiler]My posts are intended to satirize the rampant one-upsmanship in the legal profession. I don’t actually care about your genitalia you dumb retard. It is a metaphor.[/spoiler]

62278084
>>62277080
Well don’t scare me like that anon. You freaked me out since some of the people in my class do have Big Law internships and it’s kinda tough not to feel jealous sometimes with how much they’re getting paid.

62278202
>>62278084
Funposting aside, (You) should be (mildly) freaked out because spending your 1L summer split between volunteering for a professor and a one (1) month stint with a (non-federal) jjudge is concerning. Especially if the stats (You) quoted me earlier are true.

(You) are underachieving.

62278255
>>62278202
I don't know what to tell you. I applied for a billion 1L summer positions and even got a lot of interviews but none of them panned out. It would have been nice to get something more prestigious looking but I'm just glad I finally got something lined up. i have heard also that as long as you do do something legal related 1L summer then firms don't really care what it was, is that not true?

I'm not actually aiming for Big Law or anything, I'm mostly interested in environmental and real estate law and potentially being a prosecutor. I dunno I've only taken the basic classes and it feels weird this expectation to know for certain what you want to do before you've learned anything about the field.

62278315
>>62278255
I appreciate your sincerity, and I will stop frogposting.

Forget about prestige. Focus on what will get you paid. I encourage you to network with local boutique and midsize firms. These days, Big Law internships are reserved for black lesbians (unironically). You’re doing fine.

Godspeed.

62278366
>>62278315
>Forget about prestige. Focus on what will get you paid
I am getting paid for the RA job though.
>I encourage you to network with local boutique and midsize firms
This might be self-evident as a 4chan poster but I absolutely hate networking, really suck at it, and would like to avoid it as much as possible. Is it really that essential to getting a good job? Do I just have to force myself to do it? I heard the 2L summer hiring process was supposed to be way chiller and less insane.

And thanks for your advice by the way!

62278395
>>62278366
Networking is somewhat required for any career path unfortunately. My condolences anon

62280144
>>62278395

item.title: why does this keep happening — 165 replies — 57.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
why does this keep happening

Open thread • Replies: 165 • Updated: 2026-05-27 14:31 UTC

OP

>Link pumps to $10
>BTC instantly takes a nosedive

Why is Link not allowed to go above $10?

Early replies (first 3)

62263473
because fuck you, stinkie
it really all boils down to that
the entire crypto market exists JUST TO FUCK YOU STINKIES OVER
how does that make you feel
it makes me feel pic related

62263519
>>62263473
hey it's the dick guy

62263588
But Sirgay said they've decoupled from crypto prices

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62271074
>>62271066
>Link anons have a persecution bias
That's pretty normal after like 6 years of outright persecution.

>Why none from the 2017 era?
Here's one from 2018

62271088
>>62271066
>It's a moot point to think they're fudding the masses from buying the services that prediction markets or draft kings will use.
Why do you even answer to me if you don't even understand what I'm saying?
I never said anything about 'fudding' the masses (that's a freudian slip on your part).
In fact they do the opposite, they openly spoonfeed with all the marketing that CLL and Swift are doing so that anyone can buy in now while they suppress and distribute the tokens.
You are so fucking new here buddy and it shows. If you were here since day 1 you would understand that Chainlink was always supposed to be le fair crypto-native network that banks just seemed to adopt because le pristine track record and so on and so forth.
This is of course complete BS because Chainlink IS a Swift project so it's king made, but it could not seem like that for DeFi adoption to look decentralized and for their future plans which you don't seem to grasp at all.
Don't worry, I know that you are just the verbose ripple shill so you have a very sallow understanding of Chainlink..
>Why none when link was in the late 22 early 23 accumulation range
HAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHHA TAKE A LOAD OF THIS GUY

62271096
>>62271088
>>Why none when link was in the late 22 early 23 accumulation range
The ENTIRE 2022-2023 (((range))) was ENTIRELY due to BTC dump-based LINK suppression keeping the price in that range.

62271099
>>62271074
Not the gotcha you believe this is. Another lesson from trading stocks. The same people tend to trade and hold the same stocks over and over. What this means is every chart has a temperament to it. Like how there are cats, but not all cats are the same. Got it? Different temperament. So the link chart, from what you all continually show, is that links temperament is for consistent flat resistance levels. This probably won't change for the link until enough new investors/traders join the morphus blob of temperament to change it. Which is good because it lends some credibility to link repeating the same patterns so you can profit from them.

62271104
>>62271099
>Why none from the 2017 era?
>Gets shown one from the 2017 era
>Instant wall of pilpul
hahahaha

62271106
>>62271096
lel I was just about to post this.
This faggot is as blind and clueless as he is wordy and spammy.

And now he's going all new age on us:
>>62271099
>What this means is every chart has a temperament to it.

Dude's about to break out the mood crystals.

62271116
>>62271020
>And they don't give a shit about 4chan posters
you clearly do, which is why you are here calling people "schizos" for noticing BTC dumps whenever LINK gets positive news (pic rel). i dont spend a single minute discussing people in xrp threads or twitter: why would you post 26 times in this thread?

you have done this before havent you

62271138
>>62271104
>he didn't respond to my post so i'll give him another (you)
thanks cuck

>>62271106
>wordy and spammy
There's many posters and I'm trying to get each of you in the time window I have. It's slower to condense my thoughts.

>and what does this crystal do, desperate for pussy voice

>>62271116
>you clearly do
because im here all the time should i fren post you so you know
>26 times in this thread
you all keep responding and i'm conversing back
>you have done this before havent you
yeah i really like link and want good arguments for it

I'll just say this: I'm mainly hitting you all with points I could potentially see people using on your arguments here. I'm saving your incelsheets to regurgitate back if I feel like playing on your side next time and defend the idea. This back-and-forth helps me really hone in on solid points. If you actually said anything that made me genuinely question the prosecution side, I would use that the next time I defend until some new anon said another better point, and then i'd repeat the loop. In the end I'll have the best higher tier saying that is concise yet profound. You see this a lot with oldfag posts that say very little but a lot at the same time.

62271155
>>62271138
You got SCHOLED, kid.
You didn't respond because you got buckbroken from multiple angles.
You said:
>Why none from the 2017 era?
And got shown one from the 2017 era here >>62271074
You said:
>Why none when link was in the late 22 early 23 accumulation range
And got shown a lot from 2022-2023 here >>62271096
You said:
>or even the most recent link news?
And got shown one from 2025 here >>62271116

62271158
>>62271066
>>62271155
>or even the most recent link news

62271176
>>62271155
These all got posted AFTER i said "why none".
It does refute my point of all the screenshots being from the same era, but it doesn't disprove the point that it's bullshit victimizing. Also, your tone is triggered faggot. Tell me how I know our little chat is going to emotionally bother you for hours after our talk.

>>62271116
>>62271158
My response to these are going to be the same as it's been for the others. So what I'll do is watch and create the same format when this isn't true about link when news drops, and I believe we'll find many more examples of it not happening that otherwise would get ignored in favor of the emotionally charging dumps screenshot fest here. Sound fair?

62271202
When it does 10x-20x it will be quick

62271203
>>62271202
>Motley Fool
kek baggie you've been saying this for the past 6 years, enjoy reading your boomer rag

62271402
>>62271176
Now say that without crying.

62273954
>>62271176
Notice how every single "argument" you put forth was simply swept away with a relevant example of Link suppression.
Yet here you are, still trying to drown out reality with your wordswordswords.

You're going through the mother of all cognitive dissonances.

62274025
>>62269848
>A spiritual war between good and evil

62273717

>>62273459

62273913
>>62273459

>privacy

lmfao

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62273928
>>62273913
XD silly zion jews

62274700
>>62273459
Hey, can you show us the stats when they had 51%? What, they got 40% at peak and despite calling for more attacks they never managed to get that high ever again? Try again, shill.

62275020
>>62274700
>using Principia Mathematica to prove 1+1 = 2
CFB is such a midwit for this. Here. Let me show you how easy it is to prove 1+1 = 2 in the natural (read whole positive and zero) numbers using the more modern Peano axioms. Explanation added so that anyone could understand it (hopefully)
>Definition: 0 is a natural number
>Definition: let S(n) denote the successor to a natural number n s.t. S(n) = S(m) <=> n = m. Let the natural numbers be closed under S. (i.e. all natural numbers can be generated from adding 1 to 0 over and over again)
>Remark: 1 stands for S(0) and 2 for S(S(0)). (read: 1 is 0+1 and 2 is 0+1+1, this isn't the addition operator here though)
>Definition: addition of n + m:
>n 0 = n
>n S(m) = S(n + m)
>Remark: Here we are saying that the sum of any two numbers can be constructed by taking one away from the last and adding it to the first. Because all natural numbers are just 0+1*something, we are bound to reach zero. In which case we stop the process of addition and return.
>Proof: 1 + 1 = 2 by applying the definition of addition:
>1 + 1 = S(1 + 0) = S(1) = 2
QED (we're done, nothing more to show)
There. 4 statements. Principia Matematica is part of the history of mathematics, but nobody really uses its construction for the natural numbers anymore. It does appear as a piece of trivia about how hard basic mathematics really is. Which it isn't. It just takes time and effort. CFB is being incredibly obtuse on purpose here and I highly doubt he knows what half of those symbols mean in the screenshot.
>t. math major in university

62276618
>>62275020
It had some pop science fame which is probably why even knows about it in the first place.

62276635
Most monero is mined by Russian hacker botnets infecting shitty jeet pcs

62280282
>>62273459
Qubic's "51%" was literally just them lying about their hashrate

62276307
Job Posting: Goy Cattle (Entry-Level Herd)
Company: Shekelberg Global Asset ManagementLocation: The Real World™ (No Remote)
Salary: $17k + participation trophies
Type: Lifelong Indenture

About Us
We manage the money. You manage the reality. Perfect partnership.

Position Summary
Hiring motivated Goy Cattle to handle all the physical, taxable, everyday bullshit while we short futures from the penthouse.

Key Responsibilities
• Live in the Real World™ daily: traffic, kneeling, $12 eggs, and pointless cannon fodder.

• Consume goyslop, pay taxes, and reproduce below replacement rate.
• Rage on social media about culture wars while we tweak interest rates.
• Vote blue/red (both approved), stay outraged on schedule, then return to Nigflix for goyslop and real world programming.
• Die quietly after 40 years without noticing patterns.

Qualifications
• High school diploma, (college debt a plus).
• Zero pattern recognition and foreskin.
• Loves onions, forever wars, and believing the news.
• Must identify as goy. Self-ID accepted.
What We Offer
• Inflation that always wins.
• Health insurance tax (claimable if brown and jobless only).
• 1vacation days + daily slavery and holocaust infotainment .
• Premium distractions: sportsball, OnlyFans, and election theater.

Equal Opportunity
We hire all goyim — White, Black, Brown, etc. The more divided, the easier to manage.

How to ApplyEmail résumé + soul to goyim@shekelberg.globalSubject: “Ready to Graze.”

item.title: BTC is not the safe investment you think it is — 7 replies — 8.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
BTC is not the safe investment you think it is

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-05-27 16:34 UTC

OP

It was artificially crippled by limiting its throughput capability. The technical means of this is the limitation of the block size, by comparison harddrive sizes and internet speeds have increased between 15 and 50 times since 2010. For specialised companies laying their own cables it could be even more (a blockchain mining business might do that to improve their chances of winning a block and thereby earning more coins -> more revenue/profit).

Reasons for the limit could be stupidity or corruption, for example via Epstein funding certain Bitcoin developers: "are you still playing in the bitcoin space.? where are you, i have Andy Back on my island this weekend". When you send coins via Lightning it isn't your exact coins that arrive, instead someone else's coins arrive in the same quantity, this small difference is useful when you want to break the law).

As a result BTC is not a very safe investment. It is more similar to the emperror wearing no clothes than anything else. At some point people will figure it out as they try to solve technical challenges and realise they can't beat existing systems like VISA or Mastercard neither in terms of throughput (how many customers can actually use the system) nor in cost (how many customers can afford to use it). For example currently VISA can currently serve about nine hundred times as many customers as BTC.

For the time being however people still believe and the volatility means opportunity to make money. Just be aware of the risk that at some point the music will stop and you don't want to be left behind with no chair.

Early replies (first 3)

62278959
>>62278947
>BTC is
cool and orange buy now you tranny unc

62278997
>>62278947
saylor is gonna fk btc for afew years, hes literally running a ponzi on it (11.5% divvy) and hoping for the best

62279038
>>62278947
>Bitcoin is cool enough to work even on not super fast computer, and allow miners not on super fast connections (each second of latency matters)
BASED!
This is why Bitcoin is the king.
Alters will seethe.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62279130
>>62278947
bcash, the centralized scam lost, keep seething

62280024
>>62279038
Netflix works on a shitty computer because the shitty computer user is not running the system, he is simply a user. Netflix uses VERY strong server infrastructure.

This is true for all big sophisticated services.

SN said and I quote: "The design supports letting users just be users."

Letting BTC be run by shitty computers on purpose is something only a moron, useful idiot or scammer would come up with.

62280071
>>62278947
>>62280024
We’re getting close to 20 years of crypto and bitcoin in general and not a single “technologically superior” blockchain has displaced it despite there being thousands of coins with faster transaction times, greater volumes, etc. You can do it right now in fact, this afternoon. Even if you have no experience, you can hop onto Claude and in half a day create and deploy a blockchain that is technically superior to Bitcoin in every way. Let us know how it goes.
At this point if you’re still arguing about what Bitcoin was “meant to be” instead of what it is, you are retarded and also likely broke.

62280097
>>62280024

>Letting BTC be used by everyone at home on his own computer - is something only a moron, useful idiot or scammer would come up with!!!!!!!
>You will own no means to verify your money, and you will be happy!!!

item.title: Renting cars — 3 replies — 2.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Renting cars

Open thread • Replies: 3 • Updated: 2026-05-27 16:10 UTC

OP

People rent cars in my country to Uber/delivery/... drivers at $250 per week. I've been thinking of doing this with electric cars AND selling them energy. This means I could recover the money spent in the car in 1-2 years, assuming I rent them every week.
What do you think? Would this be a good idea?

Tbh I don't have much ide about cars. From what I've read, the risks are obvious: if the drivers suck or are careless, they could end up destroying the cars... My area has some rough streets also, so I'm not sure this would be a good idea outside of a big city.
Selling energy with my own private charging station would be a good business, though. I calculated it.

Early replies (first 3)

62277285
Great idea until a paki puts down the security deposit and then takes it to a chop shop to dismantle it for parts and then flees the country

62277478
>>62277285
Yeah, I guess that's the biggest risk. Guess I'll have to research insurance for this.

62280015
>>62277257
Please /biz/...

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)
